title: psilotrichum cyathuloides
intro: Psilotrichum cyathuloides, also known as the Chilean tea or Chilean mountain tea, is a flowering plant in the family Psilotrichaceae, native to the Andes mountains in South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues and digestive problems. The health benefits of Psilotrichum cyathuloides include relief from coughs, colds, and flu, as well as the alleviation of symptoms associated with bronchitis, asthma, and gastrointestinal disorders. The herbal preparations of Psilotrichum cyathuloides typically involve making a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, which can be consumed as a warm drink to reap its medicinal benefits.
